reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully captures the informal claim. "φ is a homomorphism of G onto G'" is correctly encoded as a monoid homomorphism `φ : G →* G'` together with `Function.Surjective φ` (the "onto" condition). "N ◁ G" is captured by `(N : Subgroup G) [N.Normal]`, and the conclusion "φ(N) ◁ G'" is encoded as `(Subgroup.map φ N).Normal`, where `Subgroup.map φ N` is defined to have carrier `φ '' N` (the image of N under φ). The universal quantification over arbitrary groups G, G' is expressed via the implicit type variables. Hypotheses, conclusion, and types all match exactly.
